data.text: Wait - I see the server already has its own `server/src/shared/` folder! Let me check if the client also has its own:

Great news! I checked your Neovim configuration, and you are using **LazyVim**. 

This means **`gitsigns.nvim` is already installed and pre-configured for you!** You don't need to install any new plugins.

I just updated your config (`autocmds.lua`) to add "aggressive auto-reloading." Now, the moment `opencode` edits a file in the background, your Neovim window will refresh instantly so `gitsigns` can show the new changes immediately.

### Your Workflow starting right now:

1. Let `opencode` make a change.
2. The file will automatically reload on your screen. You will see colored signs (`+`, `~`, `-`) pop up on the left edge.
3. Use your built-in LazyVim shortcuts to review and approve/reject them:
   * **`[h`** or **`]h`** : Jump up/down exactly to the lines opencode changed.
   * **`<leader>ghp`** : Preview the hunk (pops open a small window showing exactly what was added/removed).
   * **`<leader>ghs`** : Stage (Accept) the hunk.
   * **`<leader>ghr`** : Reset (Reject/Undo) the hunk.

If you don't like those specific keybindings, we can remap them to whatever you'd prefer in your `keymaps.lua` file, but those are the standard LazyVim defaults!

*(Make sure your project is inside a git repository—if you haven't run `git init` in your project folder yet, do that so `gitsigns` has something to diff against!)*

Breaking it down piece by piece:

## The command array

```ts
[
  "bunx", "postcss",               // the program to run: bunx postcss
  join(SRC, "popup", "index.css"), // input:  src/popup/index.css
  "--output", join(OUT, "popup.css"), // output: dist/popup.css
  ...(isProd ? ["--no-map"] : []), // conditionally add --no-map flag
]
```

This is the equivalent of typing this in your terminal:
```sh
bunx postcss src/popup/index.css --output dist/popup.css
```

Each element in the array is one "word" you'd type separated by spaces. You can't pass the whole command as one string — it must be split into individual parts.

---

## The spread `...(isProd ? ["--no-map"] : [])`

This is a conditional flag injection:

```ts
// if isProd is true, spreads ["--no-map"] → adds "--no-map" to the array
// if isProd is false, spreads []          → adds nothing

// dev build result:
["bunx", "postcss", "src/popup/index.css", "--output", "dist/popup.css"]

// prod build result:
["bunx", "postcss", "src/popup/index.css", "--output", "dist/popup.css", "--no-map"]
```

`--no-map` tells PostCSS to skip generating a sourcemap file in production — smaller output, no debug info needed.

---

## The options object

```ts
{ stdout: "inherit", stderr: "inherit" }
```

Controls where the child process output goes:

```
"inherit" → output flows directly to YOUR terminal (you see it live)
"pipe"    → output is captured silently into a buffer you read manually
"ignore"  → output is thrown away entirely
```

So with `"inherit"`, if PostCSS prints a warning or error, you see it immediately in the same terminal window as the build script.

---

## The full picture

```
Bun.spawnSync(command, options)
      │              │
      │              └── where to send stdout/stderr
      │
      └── ["bunx", "postcss", "input.css", "--output", "out.css"]
                 ↓
          runs this in your shell:
          bunx postcss src/popup/index.css --output dist/popup.css
```
